title: Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search
existing_short_definition: Algorithms that find the k nearest vectors in high-dimensional space much faster than exact search, by trading a small recall loss for large speedups.

title: Bi-Encoder
existing_short_definition: A retrieval architecture that encodes queries and documents independently into fixed-size vectors, allowing fast similarity lookup.

title: BM25
existing_short_definition: A classic sparse retrieval scoring function based on term frequency, inverse document frequency, and document length normalization.

title: Chunking (Document Chunking)
existing_short_definition: Splitting long documents into smaller pieces before embedding for retrieval — the single highest-leverage decision in any RAG pipeline.
existing_code_example:
# Semantic chunking with token budget
from langchain_experimental.text_splitter import SemanticChunker
from langchain_openai import OpenAIEmbeddings
splitter = SemanticChunker(
    OpenAIEmbeddings(),
    breakpoint_threshold_type='percentile',
    breakpoint_threshold_amount=85,
)
chunks = splitter.split_text(doc)

title: ColBERT (Late Interaction Retrieval)
existing_short_definition: A retrieval model that encodes each document token separately and scores a query by summing the maximum similarity per query token — late interaction.

title: Contextual Compression (Retrieval)
existing_short_definition: Passing retrieved documents through an LLM (or small model) to extract only the sentences relevant to the query before sending to the final generator.

title: Cosine Similarity
existing_short_definition: The standard metric for comparing embedding vectors — measures the angle between them, ignoring magnitude. Values range from -1 (opposite) to 1 (identical).

title: Cross-Encoder
existing_short_definition: A model that takes a query and document together as a single input and outputs a relevance score — high accuracy, expensive to use at scale.

title: Dense Retrieval
existing_short_definition: Retrieval based on embedding queries and documents into a shared vector space and using cosine similarity (or dot product) for matching.

title: FAISS
existing_short_definition: Meta's open-source library for efficient similarity search and clustering of dense vectors, the de facto reference for ANN algorithms.

title: Graph RAG
existing_short_definition: A RAG variant that builds a knowledge graph from documents and traverses entity relationships during retrieval, strong on multi-hop questions.

title: HNSW (Hierarchical Navigable Small World)
existing_short_definition: The algorithm that powers most vector databases — a multi-layer graph index that finds nearest neighbors in log(N) time instead of linear scan.
existing_code_example:
# Tune HNSW in Qdrant
from qdrant_client.models import HnswConfigDiff
client.update_collection(
    collection_name='docs',
    hnsw_config=HnswConfigDiff(m=32, ef_construct=200),
)
# At query time, raise ef for higher recall:
results = client.search(collection_name='docs', query_vector=q,
    search_params={'hnsw_ef': 128}, limit=10)

title: Hybrid Search
existing_short_definition: Retrieval that combines keyword matching (BM25) with vector similarity — catches both exact-term matches and semantic paraphrasing.
existing_code_example:
# Qdrant hybrid search
results = client.query_points(
    collection_name='docs',
    prefetch=[
        models.Prefetch(query=dense_vector, using='dense', limit=50),
        models.Prefetch(query=sparse_vector, using='sparse', limit=50),
    ],
    query=models.FusionQuery(fusion=models.Fusion.RRF),
    limit=10,
)

title: Hypothetical Document Embeddings (HyDE)
existing_short_definition: A retrieval augmentation where an LLM generates a hypothetical answer to the query, which is then embedded and used as the search vector.

title: IVF (Inverted File Index)
existing_short_definition: A vector-index structure that partitions vectors into clusters via k-means; queries search only the nearest clusters instead of the full corpus.

title: Knowledge Graph (RAG Context)
existing_short_definition: A structured representation of entities and relationships — used as a complement or alternative to vector search for multi-hop reasoning.

title: Metadata Filtering (Vector DB)
existing_short_definition: Constraining vector search to documents matching specified metadata conditions — date ranges, categories, authors, tenants — before computing similarity.

title: Multi-Query Retrieval
existing_short_definition: A retrieval strategy where an LLM generates multiple diverse rewordings of the query, retrieves for each, and merges the results.

title: Parent-Document Retriever
existing_short_definition: A retrieval pattern that indexes small chunks for precise matching but returns the larger parent document or section to the LLM for context.

title: Product Quantization (PQ)
existing_short_definition: A vector-compression technique that splits each vector into sub-vectors and quantizes each independently, shrinking index size by 4–32×.

title: Query Rewriting
existing_short_definition: Using an LLM to rewrite or refine a user's raw query into one or more retrieval-friendly queries before hitting the retriever.

title: RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation)
existing_short_definition: RAG is a technique where an AI looks up relevant information from a database before answering — like letting the AI search its own private library first.

title: RAG Fusion
existing_short_definition: A RAG pattern that combines multi-query retrieval with reciprocal rank fusion to improve recall and robustness of retrieved context.

title: Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF)
existing_short_definition: A method for merging ranked lists from multiple retrievers using each item's rank rather than raw scores — robust to score-scale mismatches.

title: Reranking
existing_short_definition: A second-pass retrieval step that uses a more expensive, more accurate model to re-score the top-K candidates from a cheap first-pass search.
existing_code_example:
# Rerank top 20 candidates
from sentence_transformers import CrossEncoder
reranker = CrossEncoder('BAAI/bge-reranker-v2-m3')
candidates = vector_store.similarity_search(query, k=20)
scores = reranker.predict([(query, c.page_content) for c in candidates])
reranked = [c for _, c in sorted(zip(scores, candidates), reverse=True)][:10]

title: Retrieval (IR)
existing_short_definition: The process of finding relevant documents or passages from a large corpus in response to a query — the first 'R' in RAG.

title: Self-Query Retrieval
existing_short_definition: A retrieval pattern where an LLM parses a natural-language query into both a semantic query and structured metadata filters.

title: Semantic Caching
existing_short_definition: Caching LLM responses based on semantic similarity of queries rather than exact string match — returning cached answers for 'similar enough' questions.

title: Sentence Window Retrieval
existing_short_definition: A retrieval pattern that indexes individual sentences but returns a window of surrounding sentences for LLM context.

title: Small-to-Big Retrieval
existing_short_definition: A retrieval pattern that matches against small embeddings for precision but returns progressively larger surrounding context to the LLM.

title: Sparse Retrieval
existing_short_definition: Retrieval based on sparse lexical representations — BM25, TF-IDF, or learned sparse models like SPLADE — matching on token overlap.

title: SPLADE (Sparse Learned Retrieval)
existing_short_definition: A retrieval model that produces sparse term-weighted representations via BERT, combining lexical interpretability with learned semantic expansion.
